chapters.text: August 27, 1972. A free outdoor concert at the Oregon Country Fair grounds in Veneta, to benefit the Springfield Creamery. The Sunshine Daydream.
China Cat into Rider — by 1972, the segue between these two had become sacred. The band hadn't missed it in over a year.
Set break. The Springfield Creamery serves ice cream to 20,000 people under the Oregon sun.
Set two opens with Playing in the Band — twenty-one minutes of the band at their most exploratory. The Oregon heat has broken. The real show begins.
Bird Song. Written for Janis Joplin, who died less than two years before this performance. Garcia's guitar will say what words cannot.
Second set break. The sun is setting over the Coast Range. The crowd settles into the grass for what comes next.
The band returns for a third set. What follows is one of the greatest Dark Stars ever performed.
Sugar Magnolia into Casey Jones into Saturday Night — the band brings it home. Three encores' worth of energy compressed into the final hour.
The Grateful Dead's Veneta show would become legendary — released as 'Sunshine Daydream' in 2013, forty-one years later.
